mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate on equities and eases financial conditions, so persistent declines in policy rates should be followed by positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not positive in a majority of months when FEDFUNDS is falling over the prior 3 months, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is near zero, further rate cuts have limited marginal effect and equity gains depend more on improving real activity; in that floor-rate regime, a low policy rate is no longer a strong bullish signal for the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations with FEDFUNDS <= 0.50% produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if adding INDPRO does not improve explanatory power over FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening household income and demand, so a monthly increase in UNRATE should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if SPX is positive in more than 60% of periods after a 0.3 pp 3-month rise in UNRATE, or if the sign of the effect flips positive in the same regime across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized labor-market deterioration, measured by higher UNRATE together with weaker PAYEMS growth, indicates recession risk and falling earnings expectations, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined UNRATE/PAYEMS signal does not outperform either series alone, or if 60-day SPX returns are not negative in at least 55% of joint-deterioration episodes.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in retail sales, captured by rising RSAFS, reflects stronger consumer demand and supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive monthly RSAFS increases are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if the signal loses significance after excluding recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals improving output and profit leverage across cyclicals, so a positive trend in INDPRO should lead to higher S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if SPX forward returns are not positive after INDPRO crosses above its 3-month moving average, or if the effect disappears when monthly revisions are excluded.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in HOUST and RSAFS marks broad demand weakening in housing and consumption, which should pressure earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls 2 months in a row and RSAFS falls 2 months in a row
failure_condition: Reject if joint two-month declines in HOUST and RSAFS are not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns at least 55% of the time, or if the combined signal is no stronger than either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: During a disinflation phase, a slower CPIAUCSL trend combined with low FEDFUNDS supports real-rate stability and easier valuation conditions, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL/FEDFUNDS combination does not predict positive 60-day SPX returns in disinflationary periods, or if the effect is absent when inflation is below its 12-month moving average.
